title: ancestry_het_kruskal_v1 — Kruskal-Wallis omnibus test of H across K cluster labels
description: One row per (K, cohort) combination. Kruskal-Wallis tests the null that heterozygosity distributions are identical across the K ancestry clusters defined by NGSadmix. Feeds the S5_kw block of the ancestry page at K=8 (and the K-sweep trajectory at S7 when aggregated across K). The corresponding paired files live under 05_ancestry_heterozygosity/tables/ for each K ∈ 2..12 and each cohort ∈ {all226, pruned81}.

K.description: Number of ancestry clusters in the NGSadmix run that produced the labels.

cohort.description: Sample subset used: all226 = full cohort; pruned81 = NAToRA-pruned (1st-degree-relative-free) subset.

statistic.description: Kruskal-Wallis H statistic (chi-square-distributed under H0 with K-1 df).

df.description: Degrees of freedom (K - 1 if all clusters have ≥1 sample).

p_value.description: Asymptotic chi-square P-value. Bonferroni / FDR adjustment is the consumer's responsibility (the table reports raw P).

n.description: Total samples included in the test (after dropping samples with missing H or cluster label).

n_clusters_tested.description: Number of clusters that actually contributed ≥1 sample. May differ from K if a small cohort × high-K cell collapses some clusters.

effect_size_eta2.description: Eta-squared effect size (H − K + 1) / (n − K). Optional; populated when the upstream R script computed it. null when not available.

_provenance.k_sweep: kruskal_results_all.tsv is the same shape concatenated across K=2..12 — see ancestry_het_kruskal_all layer.
